semanticCore: Shouting to sheep, crying out, shepherd's call, croaking of crows, or meaningless shouting

keyDerivations.meaning: To shout to sheep/cattle, cry out aloud, or croak like a crow

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَمَثَلُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي يَنْعِقُ بِمَا لَا يَسْمَعُ إِلَّا دُعَاءً وَنِدَاءً (2:171): And the example of those who disbelieve is like that of one who shouts to that which hears nothing except a call and a cry.
summaryEn: The root signifies shouting commands to animals (Naq), comparing disbelievers' blank comprehension of scripture to sheep hearing only the sound of a shepherd's voice without understanding the words.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر صياح الراعي بغنمه وزجرها فترتد صوتاً بلا فهم، وصوت الغراب (نعق)، مشبهاً الكفار في عدم فهمهم للقرآن بالدواب التي تسمع صوت الراعي ولا تفهم معناه.
rawText: نَاعِقٌ One who drives away the beasts, and cries out after them. (TA in art. زعق.) نَاعِقَآءُ : see عَانِقَآءُ. نَعَقَ بِهِمْ إِلَى الفِتْنَةٍ 1 He hallooed them on to fight, etc.: see 10 in art. عو.
